chapter goal: Show how the closing chapter opens four projective continuations: algebraic curves, oriented/discrete projective geometry, quantum projective state spaces, and dynamic geometry with complex branch tracking.
source span read: Perspectives on Projective Geometry, Chapter 27, sections 27.1-27.4; printed pages 525-556; PDF pages 547-578. Source map checked in AGENTS.md and ppg_inventory.py.
concept inventory: Higher-degree algebraic curves as homogeneous polynomial zero sets in the projective plane.
Cubic parameter count, degenerate cubics, Cayley-Bacharach-Chasles as a nine-point incidence theorem, and Pappos as a degenerate cubic case.
Bezout's theorem with multiplicity, complex intersections, and points at infinity.
Singular cubic behavior: double point, cusp, isolated real point from complex conjugate tangents.
Hessian curve and inflection points; a smooth cubic has nine projective inflection points counted over the complex projective plane.
Duality for algebraic curves via tangent-line coordinates, the polarity paradox, Pluecker corrections for double points and cusps, and tangent samples as points of the dual curve.
Oriented projective geometry: positive homogeneous scaling, covectors, chirotopes, Grassmann-Pluecker sign consistency, pseudoline arrangements, stretchability, and realizability.
Quantum state spaces: a qubit as CP1, measurement as Hermitian dual pairing, n qubits as CP(2^n-1), two-qubit entanglement measured by determinant, and nonentangled states as a conic in CP3.

Dynamic geometry systems: free and dependent elements, ambiguous primitive operations, jumping elements, construction steps as relations, analytic continuation, and complex detours around critical values.

library routing table.concept: Bezout for a line and a cubic
library routing table.representation: affine chart of a projective cubic with a generic secant and tangent line
library routing table.library: SymPy for exact polynomial degree/multiplicity checks; NumPy for numerical roots; Matplotlib for a durable labeled 2D artifact
library routing table.why this library: The source claim is algebraic and intersection-count based, so exact symbolic substitution plus an inspectable curve plot is the smallest reliable representation.
library routing table.fallback: Use only SymPy tables if the plotting backend is unavailable.

library routing table.concept: Hessian, inflection points, and tangent duality
library routing table.representation: Fermat cubic, its Hessian, projective inflection count, and sampled tangent-line map into the dual plane
library routing table.library: SymPy for Hessian determinant and exact vanishing; Matplotlib for primal curve, Hessian slice, tangent lines, and Hesse-configuration schematic
library routing table.why this library: The source explicitly names Hessians, inflection points, tangents, duality, and Pluecker degree corrections.
library routing table.fallback: Keep exact SymPy checks and render a simpler Matplotlib tangent panel.

library routing table.representation: five-point oriented configuration, separating-line sign vectors, determinant sign table, and Grassmann-Pluecker sign sanity check
library routing table.library: NumPy for determinant signs; Pandas/CSV for the signature ledger; Matplotlib for a labeled oriented arrangement
library routing table.why this library: The source's discrete section is about signs and incidence data, not metric lengths.
library routing table.fallback: Use a markdown table and JSON checks if Pandas is unavailable.

library routing table.representation: Plotly Bloch-sphere CP1 view plus two-qubit state matrices and entanglement determinant bars
library routing table.library: Plotly for interactive 3D inspection; NumPy for Hermitian probabilities and determinant invariants
library routing table.why this library: The source connects CP1, CP3, SU(2) actions, measurement probabilities, and entanglement invariants.
library routing table.fallback: Save a static Matplotlib Bloch-sphere panel and JSON determinant checks.

library routing table.representation: unit-circle/vertical-line branch paths for real motion and complex detours around a tangency singularity
library routing table.library: NumPy complex arithmetic and Matplotlib parameter-plane/branch-plane plots
library routing table.why this library: The source's example is a square-root branch problem; a complex path makes the avoided critical value visible.
library routing table.fallback: Store sampled branch coordinates as CSV and summarize the endpoint branch swap in JSON.

library routing table.representation: source-specific dependency graph linking Chapter 27 topics back to earlier projective tools
library routing table.why this library: A graph is the right artifact for a closing chapter that branches into several research directions.
library routing table.fallback: Use a table if NetworkX layout fails.

visual sequence.representation: smooth cubic with generic secant and tangent intersection multiplicities
visual sequence.learner inspection target: Compare the generic line's three intersection roots with the tangent line's double root at the point of tangency.
visual sequence.validation/invariant: Line-cubic substitution has degree 3; generic line has three complex roots; tangent substitution has root multiplicity at least 2.

visual sequence.representation: Fermat cubic with Hessian axes, nine projective inflection points, and tangent-line samples
visual sequence.learner inspection target: See that the Hessian is another curve and that tangent lines are dual coordinates, not just local decoration.
visual sequence.validation/invariant: Hessian determinant equals 216*x*y*z for the Fermat cubic; nine listed projective points satisfy both cubic and Hessian equations; sampled tangent lines contain their base points.

visual sequence.representation: oriented five-point configuration with covectors and chirotope signs
visual sequence.learner inspection target: Read zeros as incidences and signs as which side of an oriented line the other points occupy.
visual sequence.validation/invariant: Chirotope is alternating; a sample Grassmann-Pluecker relation has compensating signs; covectors include their negatives.

visual sequence.representation: CP1/Bloch sphere plus two-qubit determinant invariant under local SU(2)-type unitary actions
visual sequence.learner inspection target: Inspect phase invariance on CP1 and compare rank-one versus entangled two-qubit matrices.
visual sequence.validation/invariant: Measurement probability is invariant under global phase; absolute determinant is unchanged by local determinant-one unitary actions.

visual sequence.representation: branch paths for p=(a, +/-sqrt(1-a^2)) under real and complex parameter motion
visual sequence.learner inspection target: Observe where the real path hits tangency and how a complex detour keeps branches separated.
visual sequence.validation/invariant: Detour path has positive minimum distance from critical values a=+/-1; a full loop swaps square-root branches without collision.

visual sequence.representation: graph from earlier course tools to the chapter's four future directions
visual sequence.learner inspection target: Use the closing map to see which earlier computational languages feed each untaught topic.
visual sequence.validation/invariant: Graph has the four source section nodes and all planned artifact nodes reachable from the chapter node.

computational checks: Exact projective cubic degree and line-substitution degree checks.
Numeric and symbolic Bezout count for a generic line and tangent multiplicity for a special line.
Exact Hessian determinant for the Fermat cubic and exact vanishing for nine complex projective inflection points.
Tangent-line incidence checks using homogeneous gradients.
Covector sign-vector closure under negation for selected oriented lines and alternating determinant signs for chirotopes.
Grassmann-Pluecker sign sanity check for the oriented configuration.
Global phase invariance of qubit measurement probability.
Two-qubit determinant invariant under local determinant-one unitary transformations.
Dynamic detour avoids tangency critical values and a full loop swaps square-root branches.
Artifact existence, nonzero sizes, and nonblank pixel statistics.
proof-visualization strategy: Use invariant trackers rather than copied proofs: Bezout appears as counted roots with multiplicity; Hessian inflection points are exact common zeros; oriented matroids are determinant sign systems; entanglement is a determinant invariant under restricted local projective transformations; dynamic geometry is analytic continuation of square-root branches around critical values.
implementation notes: Revise only the Chapter 27 notebook and its matching artifact subtree.
Do not call the generic render_chapter_visuals helper.
Keep all generated paths book-local and display artifacts inline near the explanation.
Use original prose and synthetic examples; no textbook figures, screenshots, page crops, or copied exercise text.
Prefer durable PNG for static algebra/discrete/dynamic diagrams and standalone Plotly HTML for the quantum CP1/CP3 view.
risks: SymPy can become slow for general algebraic geometry, so checks use small exact cubics and determinant identities.
Plotly static export may require Kaleido, so the quantum artifact is saved as HTML.
The Hesse configuration schematic represents the incidence pattern of nine inflection points rather than a literal real drawing of all complex points.
Dynamic branch tracing is a minimal square-root model of the DGS issue, not a complete DGS implementation.
acceptance criteria: Notebook no longer depends on render_chapter_visuals or generic CP1/Mobius/cross-ratio scaffolding as the main lesson.
Notebook covers algebraic curves, Bezout, Hessians, duality/tangents, oriented/discrete projective geometry, quantum projective state spaces, and dynamic geometry.
At least six concept-named artifacts are generated under artifacts/chapter-27-what-we-did-not-touch.
Final sanity checks assert algebraic, symbolic, geometric, quantum, dynamic, and artifact invariants.
Chapter executes with validate_ppg_course.py --chapter 27 and passes scoped git diff --check.
